heading: Right of first refusal
label: 2219a Right of first refusal
notes: Amendments1988— amended section generally. children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Agricultural real estate that is acquired by an institution of the System as a result of a loan foreclosure or a voluntary conveyance by a borrower (hereinafter in this section referred to as the “previous owner”) who, as determined by the institution, does not have the financial resources to avoid foreclosure (hereinafter in this section referred to as “acquired real estate”) shall be subject to the right of first refusal of the previous owner to repurchase or lease the property, as provided in this section.

children.heading: Application of right of first refusal to sale of property
children.label: b Application of right of first refusal to sale of property
A
children.text: to purchase the property at the appraised fair market value of the property, as established by an accredited appraiser; or

children.label: B
children.text: to offer to purchase the property at a price less than the appraised value.

children.heading: Eligibility to purchase
children.label: 2 Eligibility to purchase
children.text: To be eligible to purchase the property under paragraph (1), the previous owner must, within 30 days after receiving the notice required by such paragraph, submit an offer to purchase the property.

children.heading: Mandatory sale
children.label: 3 Mandatory sale
children.text: An institution of the System receiving an offer from the previous owner to purchase the property at the appraised value shall, within 15 days after the receipt of such offer, accept such offer and sell the property to the previous owner.

children.heading: Permissive sale
children.label: 4 Permissive sale
children.text: An institution of the System receiving an offer from the previous owner to purchase the property at a price less than the appraised value may accept such offer and sell the property to the previous owner. Notice shall be provided to the previous owner of the acceptance or rejection of such offer within 15 days after the receipt of such offer.

children.heading: Rejection of offer of previous owner
children.label: 5 Rejection of offer of previous owner
i
children.text: at a price equal to, or less than, that offered by the previous owner; or

children.label: ii
children.text: on different terms and conditions than those that were extended to the previous owner,

children.heading: Notice
children.label: B Notice
children.text: Notice of the opportunity in subparagraph (A) shall be provided to the previous owner by certified mail, and the previous owner shall have 15 days in which to submit an offer to purchase the property at such price or under such terms and conditions.

children.heading: Application of right of first refusal to leasing of property
children.label: c Application of right of first refusal to leasing of property
A
children.text: to lease the property at a rate equivalent to the appraised rental value of the property, as established by an accredited appraiser; or

children.label: B
children.text: to offer to lease the property at a rate that is less than the appraised rental value of the property.

children.heading: Eligibility to lease
children.label: 2 Eligibility to lease
children.text: To be eligible to lease the property under paragraph (1), the previous owner must, within 15 days after receiving the notice required by such paragraph, submit an offer to lease the property.

children.label: A
children.text: does not have the resources available to conduct a successful farming or ranching operation; or

children.label: B
children.text: cannot meet all of the payments, terms, and conditions of such lease.

children.heading: Permissive lease
children.label: 4 Permissive lease
children.text: An institution of the System receiving an offer from the previous owner to lease the property at a rate that is less than the appraised rental value of the property may accept such offer and lease the property to the previous owner.

children.heading: Notice to previous owner
children.label: 5 Notice to previous owner
children.text: An institution of the System receiving an offer from the previous owner to lease the property at a rate less than the appraised rental value of the property shall notify the previous owner of its acceptance or rejection of the offer within 15 days after the receipt of such offer.

children.heading: Rejection of offer of previous owner
children.label: 6 Rejection of offer of previous owner
i
children.text: at a rate equal to or less than that offered by the previous owner; or

children.label: ii
children.text: on different terms and conditions than those that were extended to the previous owner,

children.heading: Notice
children.label: B Notice
children.text: Notice of the opportunity described in subparagraph (A) shall be given to the previous owner by certified mail, and the previous owner shall have 15 days after the receipt of such notice in which to agree to lease the property at such rate or under such terms and conditions.

children.heading: Notification of previous owner
children.label: 1 Notification of previous owner
children.text: If an institution of the System elects to sell or lease acquired property or a portion thereof through a public auction, competitive bidding process, or other similar public offering, the institution shall notify the previous owner, by certified mail, of the availability of the property. Such notice shall contain the minimum amount, if any, required to qualify a bid as acceptable to the institution and any terms and conditions to which such sale or lease will be subject.

children.heading: Priority
children.label: 2 Priority
children.text: If two or more qualified bids in the same amount are received by the institution under paragraph (1), such bids are the highest received, and one of the qualified bids is offered by the previous owner, the institution shall accept the offer by the previous owner.

children.heading: Nondiscrimination
children.label: 3 Nondiscrimination
children.text: No institution of the System may discriminate against a previous owner in any public auction, competitive bidding process, or other similar public offering of property acquired by the institution from such person.

children.heading: Term or condition
children.label: e Term or condition
children.text: For the purposes of this section, financing by a System institution shall not be considered to be a term or condition of a sale of acquired real estate.

children.heading: Financing
children.label: f Financing
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, a System institution shall not be required to provide financing to the previous owner in connection with the sale of acquired real estate.

children.heading: Mailing of notice
children.label: g Mailing of notice
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, each certified mail notice requirement in this section shall be fully satisfied by mailing one certified mail notice to the last known address of the previous owner.

children.heading: State laws
children.label: h State laws
children.text: The rights provided in this section shall not diminish any such right of first refusal under the law of the State in which the property is located.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: i Applicability
children.text: This section shall not apply to a bank for cooperatives.
